visual_attributes.morphology: tall, slender antelope with a long neck and limbs
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: long neck and limbs with a flat head and lyre-shaped horns

domain_specific_visual.HORN_SHAPE: lyre-shaped, curving backward then slightly forward

functional_attributes.primary_function: browser feeding on foliage of bushes and trees
secondary_functions: standing erect on hindlegs to reach high branches

domain_specific_functional.FORAGING_STRATEGY: standing on hindlegs to reach over 2 m above ground
domain_specific_functional.SOCIAL_STRUCTURE: small herds of single sex or solitary males

relationships.description: The gerenuk is native to parts of East Africa.
Gerenuks are preyed upon by African wild dogs.
Gerenuks are preyed upon by cheetahs.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Northeastern Tanzania, Kenya, Somalia, Ethiopia, Djibouti
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Known for its unique browsing technique and long neck
